query: Live Nation Ticketmaster DOJ settlement list 13 amphitheaters divestment

{"query":"Live Nation Ticketmaster DOJ settlement list 13 amphitheaters divestment","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1643,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/music/2026/03/09/live-nation-settlement/89063758007/","title":"Live Nation settles with DOJ – What it means for live entertainment fans","content":"Live Nation has agreed to settle with the department in a deal that includes the divestment of up to 13 of its amphitheaters nationwide and puts in place a 15% cap on service fees for people who want to use the venues, according to a senior Justice Department official.
The department is also seeking to rein in the company's exclusive deals with amphitheaters.
The Senate Commerce Committee had also approved and introduced the TICKET Act, a bipartisan bill that would make it a requirement to display the total event ticket price, including fees, up front.
Brian Berry, executive director of the Ticket Policy Forum, who spoke at a Senate Commerce Committee hearing on live concert and event fees, called the deal a "token tap on the wrist.""},{"url":"https://newsroom.livenation.com/statements/live-nation-entertainment-reaches-settlement-with-u-s-department-of-justice/","title":"Live Nation Entertainment Reaches Settlement With U.S. Department of Justice – Live Nation Newsroom","content":"This settlement will resolve all remaining matters with the DOJ, without any admission of wrongdoing, and the settlement will be reflected in a final proposed judgement that will be submitted to the court for approval.
Live Nation is proud to lead the way enhancing this experience with our amphitheaters, which will be open to all promoters, allowing these promoters to decide how best to distribute up to 50% of the tickets, and capping ticketing service fees at 15%.
In addition, Live Nation will be divesting its 13 exclusive booking agreements with amphitheaters nationwide.
This does not settle the claims of all plaintiffs in the lawsuit, and the company has created a $280 million settlement fund to address the states’ damages claims."},{"url":"https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/live-nation-settlement-need-to-know-next-1235528047/","title":"Live Nation Settelement: All You Need to Know and What Comes Next","content":"Well, along with needing judicial approval, Live Nation also needs to see if it can reach an agreement with all of the federal government’s co-plaintiffs: Attorneys general from nearly 40 states, plus Washington, D.C., who signed on to the suit when it was filed in 2024.
New York’s Letitia James, for instance, said it “fails to address the monopoly at the center of this case, and would benefit Live Nation at the expense of consumers.”
At a hearing Tuesday, March 10, Judge Arun Subramanian ordered the hold-out states to get back to the negotiating table with Live Nation and try to reach a deal by the end of this week.
Sure, the language will have been revised to further clarify what’s ostensibly been on the books for more than 15 years.
Instead, the company has agreed to divest from its exclusive booking arrangements with those venues, which will allow other promoters to book shows at them."},{"url":"https://www.complex.com/music/a/cmplxtara-mahadevan/live-nation-antitrust-settlement-doj","title":"Live Nation Settles Antitrust Case: Here's What It Means for Ticket Buyers","content":"Live Nation and the Department of Justice have reached a settlement in their antitrust case.
Live Nation will also be mandated to divest from at least 13 of its amphitheaters, a number that could increase.
To allow for more competition, companies like SeatGeek and Eventbrite will be able to list their tickets on Ticketmaster’s platform.
The DOJ’s biggest claims remained a part of the case, including that Live Nation’s amphitheaters are “tied” to its promotion services."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Live Nation Ticketmaster DOJ settlement list 13 amphitheaters divestment","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/music/2026/03/09/live-nation-settlement/89063758007","title":"Live Nation settles with DOJ – What it means for live entertainment fans","content":"# What the Live Nation settlement means for live entertainment fans. Live Nation and the Department of Justice have reached a settlement, after the department accused the live entertainment giant of operating an illegal monopoly over the industry. Live Nation has agreed to settle with the department in a deal that includes the divestment of up to 13 of its amphitheaters nationwide and puts in place a 15% cap on service fees for people who want to use the venues, according to a senior Justice Department official. The official added that a number of states have already signaled they would sign on to the settlement, and that the department is confident that a double-digit number of states would do so. \"It appears the DOJ has once again failed to protect live event fans and market competition with a settlement that will equate to less than a speed bump for the Live Nation-Ticketmaster monopoly,\" Berry said in a statement.","score":0.90227455,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.digitalmusicnews.com/2026/03/12/live-nation-amphitheaters","title":"Live Nation to 'Divest Ownership and/or Control' of 13 Amphitheaters","content":"# Complete List of ‘Divesting’ Live Nation Amphitheaters Emerges — Here’s What Venues Are Included. Milwaukee’s American Family Insurance Amphitheater. ## Which amphitheaters has the Justice Department ordered Live Nation to “divest” under its antitrust-action settlement? Multiple reports – and a good bit of confusion – later, here’s the complete list. “Live Nation will divest ownership and/or control of the concert venues identified in Appendix A,” the section reads. #### And Appendix A of the settlement document, for its part, includes 13 venues:. 4. American Family Insurance Amphitheater (Milwaukee, WI). #### Live Nation, in a release, indicated that it would “be divesting its 13 exclusive booking agreements with amphitheaters nationwide” – not selling off the venues, which are owned by various entities in any event, or exiting them altogether. The Ford Idaho Center, for instance, is operated by a division of Oak View Group (OVG); Live Nation, accused of having a “cozy relationship” with OVG, has been ordered to nix its exclusive-ticketing deals with the business.","score":0.875031,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://apnews.com/article/livenation-antitrust-justice-department-0a6ef66f497e5f626096de753bfff8ce","title":"Justice Department and Live Nation settle over illegal ...","content":"The term sheet also called for Ticketmaster to cap its service fees at those amphitheaters at 15% and to divest ownership or control of 13","score":0.7797419,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/09/business/live-nation-ticketmaster-doj-settlement","title":"Live Nation settles antitrust lawsuit with Justice Department | CNN Business","content":"# Live Nation settles antitrust lawsuit with Justice Department. Live Nation and Ticketmaster have reached a settlement with federal authorities over accusations the ticket giant’s business practices created a monopoly of the live events music industry. A senior justice official said in a briefing Monday that the agreement will give consumers more options and bring down prices. However, the settlement avoided a breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster, which was a demand from some of the more than 30 states suing the company. In a statement, Live Nation CEO Michael Rapino said this marks a “major step in improving the concert experience for artists and fans throughout the United States.”. “The settlement recently announced with the U.S. Department of Justice fails to address the monopoly at the center of this case, and would benefit Live Nation at the expense of consumers,” said New York Attorney General Letitia James in a statement. The previous Justice Department also wanted the company to split up Live Nation and Ticketmaster, which merged in 2010.","score":0.6186197,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/live-nation-settlement-need-to-know-next-1235528047","title":"Live Nation Settelement: All You Need to Know and What Comes Next","content":"### Everything You Need to Know About the Live Nation Settlement and What Comes Next. Live Nation has reportedly reached a tentative settlement with the Department of Justice that would require Live Nation, the owner of Ticketmaster, to pay around $200 million in damages. The most significant music-industry trial in recent history was abruptly cut short Monday when Live Nation announced that it had settled its antitrust case with the Department of Justice. The tentative deal will force Live Nation and Ticketmaster to make several changes to their business model, which could open up avenues for more competition in the live-entertainment space. And many of Live Nation and Ticketmaster’s fiercest critics maintain that the settlement falls far short of what they say is truly necessary: A break-up of the behemoth that dominates the worlds of ticketing, venues, and promotions. New York’s Letitia James, for instance, said it “fails to address the monopoly at the center of this case, and would benefit Live Nation at the expense of consumers.”.","score":0.54724693,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.79,"request_id":"31ec1fe8-379e-4d76-aa4c-d25a1288b67a"}
